network.overall_strategy: The synthesis network integrates the three primary literature-proposed routes for prebiotic arginine formation: (1) cyanosulfidic protometabolism producing an α-aminonitrile precursor via guanylation of β-aminopropionaldehyde (Patel et al., 2015; Tsanakopoulou and Sutherland, 2017), (2) direct guanylation of ornithine by cyanamide (Schulze and Winterstein, 1899; Tsubokura et al., 2014), and (3) a hypothetical urea cycle analog starting from glutamic acid via ornithine and citrulline using cyanate carbamoylation (Ferris et al., 1978; Juarez et al., 2021). Precursors like glutamic acid and ornithine are sourced from hydrothermal and surface environments (Johnson et al., 2008; Parker et al., 2011), while cyanamide forms via surface UV photochemistry (Schimpl et al., 1965). Multiple convergent pathways address synthesis challenges by providing redundancy, with ornithine, cyanamide, and β-aminopropionaldehyde as hubs. Environmental flow: Hydrothermal produces basic amino acids (glutamate, ornithine precursors); Surface drives cyanosulfidic and guanylation steps; Biochemical handles final assembly and hydrolysis. All reactions cite supporting literature; speculative connections (e.g., ornithine formation details) are minimized and justified.

reactions.name: Cyanamide formation from HCN and NH3 via UV photochemistry
reactions.mechanism: UV-driven photolysis of HCN in NH3/H2O mixtures forms cyanamide via intermediate formamide tautomerization.
reactions.reasoning: Cyanamide forms from UV irradiation of CH4/NH3/H2O mixtures, extended to HCN as precursor (Schimpl et al., 1965). TiO2 enhances photochemistry (Negrón-Mendoza et al., 2012).

reactions.name: Acrylonitrile synthesis via HCN coupling
reactions.mechanism: Cu(I)-catalyzed cross-coupling of HCN with acetylene (implied from HCN/H2S), but simplified to HCN/H2S under UV.
reactions.reasoning: Directly from cyanosulfidic pathway: Cu(I)-catalyzed HCN + acetylene → acrylonitrile (Patel et al., 2015).

reactions.name: β-Aminopropionitrile from acrylonitrile amination
reactions.mechanism: Nucleophilic addition of NH3 to acrylonitrile's nitrile group, catalyzed by clay adsorption.
reactions.reasoning: Acrylonitrile + NH3 → β-aminopropionitrile in cyanosulfidic network (Patel et al., 2015). Montmorillonite concentrates reactants (Catalano et al., 2024).

reactions.name: Reduction to β-aminopropionaldehyde
reactions.mechanism: Photoreductive cleavage of nitrile to aldehyde on pyrite surface.
reactions.reasoning: Photoreduction of β-aminopropionitrile → β-aminopropionaldehyde (Patel et al., 2015). Pyrite catalyzes reductive amination analogs (Zhang et al., 2024; Cleaves et al., 2010).

reactions.name: Guanylation of β-aminopropionaldehyde to arginine precursor
reactions.mechanism: Primary amine of β-aminopropionaldehyde attacks cyanamide's carbon, forming guanidine linkage; further HCN addition and reduction.
reactions.reasoning: Especially efficient guanylation: β-aminopropionaldehyde + cyanamide → arginine α-aminonitrile precursor (compound 46) (Patel et al., 2015; Tsanakopoulou and Sutherland, 2017). Montmorillonite protects cationic intermediates (Catalano et al., 2024).

reactions.name: Hydrolysis of arginine precursor to arginine
reactions.mechanism: Aminonitrile hydrolysis to α-amino acid under aqueous conditions.
reactions.reasoning: Hydrolysis of α-aminonitrile precursor → arginine (Patel et al., 2015).

reactions.name: Glutamic acid synthesis via hydrothermal CO2 reduction
reactions.mechanism: CO2 reduction and amination on greigite surface to form α-amino acids including glutamic acid.
reactions.reasoning: Glutamic acid abundant in hydrothermal simulations and Miller experiments (Johnson et al., 2008; Amend and Shock, 1998; Kitadai, 2015). Greigite catalyzes CO2 reduction to amino acid precursors (Jordan et al., 2021).

reactions.name: Ornithine from glutamic acid via decarboxylation and transamination
reactions.mechanism: Thermal decarboxylation of glutamic acid side chain followed by reductive amination to ornithine.
reactions.reasoning: Ornithine derived from glutamate in prebiotic analogs; detected in spark discharge (Johnson et al., 2008; Parker et al., 2011). Magnetite enables Fischer-Tropsch-type reductions (Huber and Wächtershäuser, 1998). Speculative connection justified to link to urea cycle analog.

reactions.name: Direct guanylation of ornithine to arginine
reactions.mechanism: δ-Amino group of ornithine nucleophilically attacks cyanamide, forming guanidinium group.
reactions.reasoning: Ornithine + cyanamide → arginine via guanylation (Schulze and Winterstein, 1899; Tsubokura et al., 2014). Montmorillonite concentrates and catalyzes (Catalano et al., 2024; Zhou et al., 2019).

reactions.name: Cyanate formation from HCN hydrolysis
reactions.mechanism: Partial hydrolysis of HCN to cyanate ion.
reactions.reasoning: Cyanate from HCN oligomers (Ferris et al., 1978). Borate stabilizes in surface pools.

reactions.name: Citrulline from ornithine carbamoylation
reactions.mechanism: Ornithine δ-amine reacts with cyanate to form carbamoyl linkage.
reactions.reasoning: Ornithine → citrulline via cyanate carbamoylation in urea cycle analog (Ferris et al., 1978; Juarez et al., 2021). Citrulline identified in HCN hydrolysates.

reactions.name: Arginine from citrulline via guanidination
reactions.mechanism: Citrulline reacts with cyanamide to install full guanidinium group.
reactions.reasoning: Hypothetical extension of urea cycle analog to arginine using cyanamide (Juarez et al., 2021). Justified as necessary to complete route from literature precursors.

pathways.name: Cyanosulfidic core route
pathways.description: Direct cyanosulfidic protometabolism to arginine precursor then hydrolysis.

pathways.name: Urea cycle analog via citrulline
pathways.description: Hydrothermal glutamate to ornithine, surface cyanate, biochemical assembly.

pathways.name: Ornithine guanylation with surface cyanamide
pathways.description: Hydrothermal ornithine + surface cyanamide direct guanylation.

pathways.name: Extended cyanosulfidic with guanylation variant
pathways.description: β-Aminopropionitrile reduction and guanylation, alternative to full HCN addition.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal-surface interconnection via ornithine
pathways.description: Glutamate to ornithine hydrothermally, guanylation on surface clay.

pathways.name: Cyanate-assisted urea cycle with cyanamide finish
pathways.description: Ornithine to citrulline, then cyanamide guanidination.

pathways.name: Surface-dominated cyanosulfidic variant
pathways.description: Full surface synthesis bypassing hydrothermal for ornithine equivalent.

pathways.name: Biochemical assembly from mixed precursors
pathways.description: Hydrothermal ornithine + surface cyanate/cyanamide to arginine via citrulline.

pathways.name: Convergent guanylation from β-aldehyde and ornithine
pathways.description: Parallel surface routes converging at guanylation step.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Core cyanosulfidic route aligns closely with Patel et al. (2015), but acrylonitrile formation via H2S is not directly supported.
suggestions: Replace H2S with acetylene or specify Cu catalyst for acrylonitrile synthesis.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Integrates hydrothermal ornithine with surface guanylation, supported by Johnson et al. (2008), but ornithine formation is multi-step.
suggestions: Detail transamination mechanism with mineral catalysts like iron-sulfur.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Urea cycle analog hypothetical per Ferris et al. (1978), cyanate formation inaccurate.
suggestions: Use CO + NH3 for cyanate; cite Juarez et al. (2021) for stability.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Direct guanylation aligns with Schulze (1899) and Tsubokura (2014), good redundancy.
suggestions: Specify Sc(OTf)3 or mineral catalyst for guanylation.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Extended variant with guanylation convergence, but overlaps routes effectively.
suggestions: Clarify variant guanylation step with literature reference.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Strong hydrothermal-surface flow via ornithine hub, justifies transitions well.
suggestions: Add mineral agents like greigite for hydrothermal steps.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Cyanate-assisted with cyanamide finish, addresses urea cycle challenges.
Surface-dominated, aligns with UV photochemistry in pools.
suggestions: Specify UV conditions and mineral catalysts.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Biochemical assembly from mixed precursors, good for final stage.
Convergent from β-aldehyde and ornithine, enhances redundancy.
suggestions: Balance steps with convergence point details.

evaluation.network_feedback: Excellent integration of three literature routes with redundancy addressing arginine challenges; minor issues in specific reaction mechanisms like acrylonitrile and cyanate formation, but overall chemically sound and aligned with Patel (2015), Schulze (1899), and Ferris (1978). Environmental flow is realistic, though mineral agents under-specified.
